chunks.focus: SkillForge C++ Signal Check - Concepts, Output Tracing, and Debugging

questions.question: If the class name is X, what is the type of the `this` pointer inside a non-static, non-const member function?
options: A) const X* const
B) X* const
C) X*
D) X&
questions.correctAnswer: B) X* const

options: A) n is zero / The square of n is 25
B) n is not zero / The square of n is 25
C) n is not zero / The square of n is 0
D) Compile-time error because assignment is not allowed in conditions
questions.correctAnswer: C) n is not zero / The square of n is 0

questions.question: Given `char message[21];` and user input `Please go away.`, what does `cin >> message;` store in `message`?
options: A) Please
B) Please go away.
C) Please go
D) It always causes a compile-time error
questions.correctAnswer: A) Please

questions.question: What is wrong with this loop header? `for (int k = 2, k <= 12, k++)`
options: A) The increment should always be `++k`
B) The loop variable must be named `i`
C) The commas should be semicolons
D) The header needs braces around it
questions.correctAnswer: C) The commas should be semicolons

questions.question: What is `*ptr++` equivalent to?
options: D) `++ptr`

questions.question: When is `std::bad_alloc` typically thrown?
options: A) When `new` cannot allocate memory
B) When a destructor runs twice
C) When a cast between related classes fails
D) When a reference is left uninitialized
questions.correctAnswer: A) When `new` cannot allocate memory

questions.question: Why does RAII matter in modern C++?
options: A) It ties resource lifetime to object lifetime for safer cleanup
B) It makes C++ dynamically typed
C) It removes the need for destructors
D) It is only useful in graphics programming
questions.correctAnswer: A) It ties resource lifetime to object lifetime for safer cleanup

questions.question: What problem do smart pointers help solve?
options: A) Expressing ownership and reducing manual memory management mistakes
B) Replacing templates with runtime polymorphism
C) Avoiding the need for constructors
D) Turning stack allocation into garbage collection
questions.correctAnswer: A) Expressing ownership and reducing manual memory management mistakes

questions.question: How do you declare an abstract class in C++?
options: A) Mark the class with the keyword `abstract`
B) Give the class at least one pure virtual function
C) Mark all data members as `virtual`
D) Inherit privately from `std::exception`
questions.correctAnswer: B) Give the class at least one pure virtual function

questions.question: Which description best matches an interface-style class in C++?
options: A) A class with only static methods
B) A class whose methods are all pure virtual
C) A class declared with the keyword `interface`
D) A class with no constructor
questions.correctAnswer: B) A class whose methods are all pure virtual

questions.question: If class `B` publicly inherits from class `A`, in what order do constructors run when a `B` object is created?
options: A) `B` first, then `A`
B) `A` first, then `B`
C) Only `B` runs
D) The order is undefined
questions.correctAnswer: B) `A` first, then `B`

questions.question: What can happen if a pointer is deleted twice?
options: A) Nothing; repeated `delete` is always safe
B) The object is recreated automatically
C) It can trigger undefined behavior and crash the program
D) The compiler always prevents it
questions.correctAnswer: C) It can trigger undefined behavior and crash the program

questions.question: Which cast is designed for conversions between pointers or references to related polymorphic classes?
options: A) `reinterpret_cast`
B) `dynamic_cast`
C) `const_cast`
D) `decltype_cast`
questions.correctAnswer: B) `dynamic_cast`

questions.question: What is the usual role of a virtual table (vtable) in C++ implementations?
options: A) A dynamic table created per object at runtime
B) A static table typically associated with each polymorphic class
C) A stack frame used during recursion
D) A container used by the STL
questions.correctAnswer: B) A static table typically associated with each polymorphic class

options: D) Syntax error

questions.question: A function `sum_from_to(first, last)` returns the sum of all integers from `first` to `last` inclusive, even when `first > last`. What should `sum_from_to(7, 4)` return?
A function `enough(goal)` returns the smallest positive integer `n` such that `1 + 2 + ... + n` is at least `goal`. What should `enough(9)` return?
Why is the Standard Template Library important in C++?
options: A) It provides reusable containers, algorithms, and utilities for common tasks
B) It only matters for GUI applications
C) It replaces the compiler
D) It removes the need to learn pointers
questions.correctAnswer: A) It provides reusable containers, algorithms, and utilities for common tasks

questions.question: Why are compiler warnings and sanitizers valuable in C++ projects?
options: A) They catch important classes of bugs early during development and testing
B) They guarantee zero runtime bugs in production
C) They replace the need for debugging
D) They only help with formatting
questions.correctAnswer: A) They catch important classes of bugs early during development and testing

chunks.focus: Advanced C++ Reasoning - Ownership, STL, Templates, and Systems

questions.question: Why should a polymorphic base class often have a virtual destructor?
options: A) So objects can be copied faster
B) So deleting through a base pointer destroys the derived object correctly
C) So the class becomes abstract automatically
D) So constructors can be overloaded
questions.correctAnswer: B) So deleting through a base pointer destroys the derived object correctly

questions.question: What is the key ownership meaning of `std::unique_ptr`?
options: A) Shared ownership across many objects
B) Unique ownership with transfer via move
C) It can only manage arrays
D) It is copied whenever passed to a function
questions.correctAnswer: B) Unique ownership with transfer via move

questions.question: When is `std::shared_ptr` most appropriate?
options: A) When multiple owners must keep a resource alive
B) When stack allocation is required
C) When you never want reference counting
D) When you need deterministic copy elision
questions.correctAnswer: A) When multiple owners must keep a resource alive

questions.question: Why can cyclic references be dangerous with `std::shared_ptr`?
options: A) They make references const
B) They can keep reference counts above zero and leak memory
C) They force stack allocation
D) They disable polymorphism
questions.correctAnswer: B) They can keep reference counts above zero and leak memory

questions.question: What is the purpose of `std::weak_ptr`?
options: A) To own an object more cheaply than `unique_ptr`
B) To hold a non-owning reference that can break `shared_ptr` cycles
C) To replace raw pointers in all cases
D) To allocate memory on the stack
questions.correctAnswer: B) To hold a non-owning reference that can break `shared_ptr` cycles

questions.question: Why is `const T&` commonly used as a function parameter?
options: A) It always makes a deep copy
B) It avoids copying while preventing modification of the argument
C) It can only be used with primitive types
D) It turns the function into a template
questions.correctAnswer: B) It avoids copying while preventing modification of the argument

questions.question: What does `std::move` actually do?
options: A) It physically moves bytes to a new memory address
B) It casts an expression to an rvalue so move operations can be used
C) It deletes the source object
D) It guarantees zero-cost transfer in every case
questions.correctAnswer: B) It casts an expression to an rvalue so move operations can be used

questions.question: Why can `std::vector` be more cache-friendly than a linked list?
options: A) Because vectors are always smaller
B) Because vector elements are stored contiguously in memory
C) Because vectors use recursion internally
D) Because linked lists cannot store objects
questions.correctAnswer: B) Because vector elements are stored contiguously in memory

questions.question: What is a common consequence of `std::vector` reallocation during growth?
options: A) Iterators and references to elements may be invalidated
B) Elements become immutable
C) The vector becomes thread-safe
D) Capacity must always shrink
questions.correctAnswer: A) Iterators and references to elements may be invalidated

questions.question: What average-case lookup complexity does `std::unordered_map` aim for?
options: A) O(n)
B) O(log n)
D) O(n log n)

questions.question: Why are templates powerful in C++?
options: A) They allow generic code to be instantiated for different compatible types at compile time
B) They remove the need for type checking
C) They only work for containers
D) They are runtime-only constructs
questions.correctAnswer: A) They allow generic code to be instantiated for different compatible types at compile time

questions.question: What problem do header guards or `#pragma once` help prevent?
options: A) Runtime memory leaks
B) Multiple inclusion of the same header during compilation
C) Deadlocks in multithreaded code
D) Dynamic linking errors at runtime
questions.correctAnswer: B) Multiple inclusion of the same header during compilation

questions.question: What is undefined behavior in C++?
options: A) Behavior guaranteed to throw an exception
B) Behavior for which the C++ standard imposes no requirements
C) A warning generated by the preprocessor
D) A feature available only in debug builds
questions.correctAnswer: B) Behavior for which the C++ standard imposes no requirements

questions.question: Why is data-race-free code important in multithreaded C++?
options: A) Because data races can lead to undefined behavior
B) Because threads cannot share data at all
C) Because mutexes only work in release mode
D) Because race conditions are only performance issues
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because data races can lead to undefined behavior

questions.question: What is a deadlock?
options: A) A compilation error caused by circular includes
B) A state where threads wait on each other indefinitely
C) A memory leak caused by recursion
D) An optimization technique for mutexes
questions.correctAnswer: B) A state where threads wait on each other indefinitely

questions.question: Why can exception-safe resource management be easier with RAII?
options: A) Destructors run automatically during stack unwinding
B) Exceptions disable destructors
C) RAII prevents all exceptions from being thrown
D) It removes the need for scopes
questions.correctAnswer: A) Destructors run automatically during stack unwinding

questions.question: What is the benefit of using `auto` in modern C++ when used carefully?
options: A) It makes the language dynamically typed
B) It can reduce verbosity while preserving static typing and compiler deduction
C) It always improves runtime performance
D) It disables templates
questions.correctAnswer: B) It can reduce verbosity while preserving static typing and compiler deduction

questions.question: What does a lambda expression provide in C++?
options: A) A preprocessor macro
B) An anonymous function object that can capture local variables
C) A replacement for classes
D) A type-erased pointer
questions.correctAnswer: B) An anonymous function object that can capture local variables

questions.question: What is a common reason to prefer namespaces?
options: A) They prevent all linking errors
B) They help organize code and avoid name collisions
C) They automatically inline every function
D) They replace header files
questions.correctAnswer: B) They help organize code and avoid name collisions

questions.question: What does the Rule of Five suggest?
options: A) Every class must have exactly five data members
B) If a class manages resources and defines one special member like copy/move/destructor, it may need the others too
C) You should only write five functions per file
D) Inheritance should never exceed five levels
questions.correctAnswer: B) If a class manages resources and defines one special member like copy/move/destructor, it may need the others too

questions.question: Which operator is used for pointer dereferencing in C++?
